publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-10-19 23:31:16 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Shannon Engineering, Inc., records
did.abstract: Collection consists of engineering reports and drawings covering 44 different airplane types, 6 helicopter types, and 11 engine types. Includes documentation on hush kits for noise abatement of the Boeing 707 and others, critical structural modifications of the 747 for heavy loads, testing Sikorsky helicopters to accept an 11,000 pound external load and photographic studies taken in flight. In addition, reports touch on regulatory matters, and compliance with evolving requirements of the F.A.A. and its international peers. The collection encompases material during a transitional time period - analog technology to computer/electronic breakthroughs developed during the space program to the digital age and Internet.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of engineering reports and drawings covering 44 different airplane types, 6 helicopter types, and 11 engine types. Includes documentation on hush kits for noise abatement of the Boeing 707 and others, critical structural modifications of the 747 for heavy loads, testing Sikorsky helicopters to accept an 11,000 pound external load and photographic studies taken in flight. In addition, reports touch on regulatory matters, and compliance with evolving requirements of the F.A.A. and its international peers. The collection encompases material during a transitional time period - analog technology to computer/electronic breakthroughs developed during the space program to the digital age and Internet.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Shannon Engineering, Inc., records, #8746. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Original notes from Shannon Engineering, Inc. inventory list were transcribed as Scope & ContentN otes for each file. Several gaps in the numerical sequence exist where files were not present. These files were indicated by Shannon Engneering, Inc. as being unused project numbers. These numbers are JS1416-1419, 1458, 1462, 1464, 1469, and 1478.
